A class to represent a file in AAVSO extended format.

Parameters
----------
obscode : str
    The observer's code.

Attributes
----------
obscode : str
    The observer's code.

type : str
    The type of observation. Defaults to 'EXTENDED'.

software : str
    The software used to generate the file. Defaults to 'stellarphot'.

delim : str
    The delimiter used in the file. Defaults to ','.

date_format : str
    The format of the date. Defaults to 'JD'.

date : `astropy.table.Column`
    The date/time of the observation in the format `date_format`.

obstype : str
    The type of observation. Defaults to 'CCD'.

magnitude : `astropy.table.Column`
    The magnitude of the observation.

magerr : `astropy.table.Column`
    The error in the magnitude of the observation.

filter : `astropy.table.Column`
    The filter/band used for the observation. Must be one of the AAVSO passbands,
    which are listed at https://www.aavso.org/filters.

kmag : `astropy.table.Column`
    The magnitude of the check star.

cmag : `astropy.table.Column`
    The magnitude of the comparison star.

ensemble : bool
    Whether the observation is an ensemble observation. Defaults to False.

group : int
    The group number of the ensemble observation. Defaults to 0.

trans : str
    Whether the observation is a transformed observation. Defaults to 'NO'.

chart : str
    The chart used for the observation. Defaults to ''.

notes : str
    Notes about the observation. Defaults to ''.

starid : str
    The AUID of the star. Defaults to ''.

cname : str
    The AUID of the comparison star. Defaults to ''.

kname : str
    The AUID of the check star. Defaults to ''.

airmass : `astropy.table.Column`
    The airmass of the observation.

mtype : str
    The type of magnitude. Defaults to 'STD'.

Fit a 2D Gaussian to data.

Written as a replacement for functionality that was removed from
photutils.

This function will be kept private so we don't have to support it.

Copy/pasted from
https://github.com/astropy/photutils/pull/1064/files#diff-9e64908ff7ac552845b4831870a749f397d73c681d218267bd9087c7757e6dd4R285

Parameters
----------

    The 2D array of data to fit.

Returns
-------

    The best-fit 2D Gaussian model.

functions.name: stellarphot.source_detection.compute_fwhm
documentation:
Computes the FWHM in both x and y directions of sources in an image.

# Parameters

ccd

: The CCD Image array.

sources

: An astropy table of the positions of sources in the image.

fwhm_estimate

: The initial guess for the FWHM of the sources in the image.

x_column

: The name of the column in `sources` that contains the x positions
  of the sources.

y_column

: The name of the column in `sources` that contains the y positions
  of the sources.

fit

: If `True`, fit a 2D Gaussian to each source to estimate its FWHM. If
  `False`, estimate the FWHM of each source by computing the second
  moments of its light distribution using photutils.

sky_per_pix_avg

: Sky background to subtract before centroiding.

# Returns

fwhm_x, fwhm_y

: The FWHM of each source in the x and y directions.

functions.name: stellarphot.source_detection.source_detection
documentation:
Returns an astropy table containing the position of sources
within the image.

# Parameters

ccd

: The CCD Image array.

fwhm

: Full-width half-max of stars in the image.

sigma

: The number of standard deviations to use as the lower and
  upper clipping limit.

iters

: The number of iterations to perform sigma clipping

threshold

: The absolute image value above which to select sources.

find_fwhm

: If `True`, estimate the FWHM of each source by fitting a 2D Gaussian
  to it. If `False`, estimate the FWHM of each source by computing
  the second moments of its light distribution.

sky_per_pix_avg

: Sky background to subtract before centroiding.

# Returns

sources: `astropy.table.Table`

: A table of the positions of sources in the image.  If `find_fwhm` is
  `True`, includes a column called `FWHM`.

documentation:
Initializes a Camera object instance.

Parameters
----------
gain : `astropy.quantity.Quantity`
    The gain of the camera in units such that the unit of the product `gain`
    times the image data matches the unit of the `read_noise`.

read_noise : `astropy.quantity.Quantity`
    The read noise of the camera in units of electrons.

dark_current : `astropy.quantity.Quantity`
    The dark current of the camera in units such that, when multiplied by
    exposure time, the unit matches the unit of the `read_noise`.

A class to represent a CCD-based camera.

# Attributes

gain

: The gain of the camera in units such that the unit of the product
  `gain` times the image data matches the unit of the `read_noise`.

read_noise

: The read noise of the camera in units of electrons.

dark_current

: The dark current of the camera in units such that, when multiplied
  by exposure time, the unit matches the unit of the `read_noise`.

# Notes

The gain, read noise, and dark current are all assumed to be constant
across the entire CCD.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from astropy import units as u
>>> from stellarphot import Camera
>>> camera = Camera(gain=1.0 * u.electron / u.adu,
...                 read_noise=1.0 * u.electron,
...                 dark_current=0.01 * u.electron / u.second)
>>> camera.gain
<Quantity 1. electron / adu>
>>> camera.read_noise
<Quantity 1. electron>
>>> camera.dark_current
<Quantity 0.01 electron / s>
```

functions.name: stellarphot.photometry.calculate_noise
documentation:
Computes the noise in a photometric measurement.

This function computes the noise (in units of gain) in a photometric measurement using the
revised CCD equation from Collins et al (2017) AJ, 153, 77.  The equation is:

$$
\sigma = \sqrt{G \cdot F + A \cdot \left(1 + \frac{A}{B}\right)\cdot \left[ G\cdot S + D \cdot t + R^2 + (0.289 G)^2\right]}
$$

where $\sigma$ is the noise, $G$ is the gain, $F$ is the flux,
$A$ is the aperture area in pixels, $B$ is the annulus area in pixels,
$S$ is the sky per pixel, $D$ is the dark current per second,
$R$ is the read noise, and $t$ is exposure time.

Note: The $(0.289 G)^2$ term is "digitization noise" and is optional.

# Parameters

gain

: Gain of the CCD. In units of electrons per DN.

read_noise

: Read noise of the CCD in electrons.

dark_current_per_sec

: Dark current of the CCD in electrons per second.

flux

: Flux of the source in electrons.

sky_per_pix

: Sky per pixel in electrons.

aperture_area

: Area of the aperture in pixels.

annulus_area

: Area of the annulus in pixels.

exposure

: Exposure time in seconds.

include_digitization

: Whether to include the digitization noise. Defaults to False.

# Returns

noise

: The noise in the photometric measurement.

functions.name: stellarphot.differential_photometry.magnitude_transforms.filter_transform
documentation:
Transform SDSS magnitudes to BVRI using either the transforms from
Jester et al or Ivezic et al.

# Parameters

mag_data

: Table containing `g`, `r` and `i` magnitudes (or at least)
  those required to transform to the desired output filter.

output_filter

: Filter for which magnitude should be calculated. Note that
  *case matters* here.

g, r, i

: Name of column in table for that magnitude.

transform

: Transform equations to use.

# Returns

`astropy.table.Column`

: Output transformed magnitudes as a table column

# Notes

The transforms implemented in this function are taken from:

Jester, et al, *The Sloan Digital Sky Survey View of the Palomar-Green Bright Quasar Survey*, AJ 130, p. 873 (2005)
<http://iopscience.iop.org/article/10.1086/432466/meta>

Ivezić et al, *A Comparison of SDSS Standard Star Catalog for Stripe 82 with Stetson's Photometric Standards*,
The Future Of Photometric, Spectrophotometric And Polarimetric Standardization, ASP Conference Series 364, p. 165 (2007)
<http://aspbooks.org/custom/publications/paper/364-0165.html>

functions.name: stellarphot.differential_photometry.magnitude_transforms.calculate_transform_coefficients
documentation:
Calculate linear transform coefficients from input magnitudes to catalog
magnitudes.

# Parameters

input_mag

: Input magnitudes; for example, instrumental magnitudes.

catalog_mag

: Catalog (or reference) magnitudes; the magnitudes to which the
  input_mag will eventually be transformed.

color

: Colors to use in determining transform coefficients.

input_mag_error

: Error in input magnitudes. Default is zero.

catalog_mag_error

: Error in catalog magnitudes. Default is zero.

faintest_mag_for_transform

: If this is not `None`, the magnitude of the faintest catalog stars
  to use in computing transform coefficients.

order

: Order of the polynomial fit to use in correcting for color.

sigma

: Value of sigma to use to reject outliers while fitting using
  sigma clipping.

gain

: If not `None`, adjust the instrumental magnitude by
  -2.5 * log10(gain), i.e. gain correct the magnitude.

verbose

: If `True`, print some diagnostic information.

extended_output

: If `True`, return additional information.

# Returns

filtered_data

: The data, with the mask set `True` for the data that was *omitted*
  from the fit.

model

: Entries in the model are the coefficients in the fit made to the
  data. Since the model is always a polynomial, these are terms in
  a polynomial in the order of ascending power. In other words, the
  coefficient `ci` is the coefficient of the term `x**i`.

If `extended_output=True`, then also return:

fit_input

: A tuple of color, magnitude for only the stars brighter than
  `faintest_mag_for_transform`. These are input to the sigma-clipping
  fitter.

used_in_fit

: A tuple of color, magnitude for only the stars brighter than
  `faintest_mag_for_transform` that were not sigma-cliped out.

# Notes

This function has some pretty serious limitations right now:

- Errors in the independent variable are ignored.
- Outliers are rejected using a modified loss function (Huber loss) that
  cannot be modified.
- No errors are estimated in the calculated transformation coefficients.

And there is all the stuff that is not listed here...

functions.name: stellarphot.differential_photometry.magnitude_transforms.transform_magnitudes
documentation:
Calculate catalog magnitudes and transform coefficients
from instrumental magnitudes.

# Parameters

input_mags

: Table which contains a column with instrumental magnitudes, i.e.
  -2.5 * log10(net_counts / exposure_time).

catalog

: Table containing reference catalog of magnitudes and colors.

transform_catalog

: Table containing the reference catalog of magnitudes and colors
  to use in determining the transform coefficients. Can be the
  same table as `catalog` if desired.

input_mag_column

: Name of the column in `input_mags` with the magnitudes to be
  transformed.

catalog_mag_column

: Name of the column in `catalog` with the reference magnitude.

catalog_color_column

: Name of the column in `catalog` with color for each star in the
  catalog.

faintest_mag_for_transform

: If this is not `None`, the magnitude of the faintest catalog stars
  to use in computing transform coefficients.

sigma

: Number of standard deviations to use in rejecting outliers when fitting
  using sigma clipping.

order

: Order of the polynomial to use in fitting magnitude difference/color
  relationship.

gain

: If not `None`, adjust the instrumental magnitude by
  -2.5 * log10(gain), i.e. gain correct the magnitude.

# Returns

our_cat_mags

: The calculated catalog magnitudes for the stars in `input_mags`.

good_match_all

: Boolean array indicating which stars in `input_mags` have a match
  in the catalog.

transforms

: The coefficients of the transform. The coefficients are in the order
  of ascending power, i.e. the coefficient `ci` is the coefficient
  of the term `x**i`.  Warning: This returns a namedtuple if the fit
  fails.

functions.name: stellarphot.differential_photometry.magnitude_transforms.transform_to_catalog
documentation:
Transform a set of intrumental magnitudes to a standard system using either
instrumental colors or catalog colors.

# Parameters

observed_magnitudes_grouped

: An astropy table, grouped by whatever you want that sepearates the data into
  data from just one image. BJD of the center of the observatory is one reasonable choice

obs_mag_col

: Name of the column in `observed_magnitudes_grouped` that contains instrumental
  magnitudes.

obs_filter

: Name of the filter in which observations were done. Should be one of the names at
  <https://www.aavso.org/filters>

obs_error_column

: Name of the column in `observed_magnitudes_grouped` that contains the error in the magnitude.

cat_filter

: Name of the filter/passband in catalog that should be matched to the instrumental magnitudes.

cat_color

: Names of the two columns in the caatalog that should be used to calculate color. The magnitude
  difference will be calculated in the ortder the fitlers are given. For example, if the value is
  `('r_mag', 'i_mag')` then the calculated color will be the `r_mag` column minus
  the `i_mag` column.

a_delta, b_delta, c_delta, d_delta

: Range allowed in fitting for each of the parameters `a`, `b`, `c`, and `d`. Use `1E-6` to fix a parameter.

a_cen

: Center of range for the fitting parameter `a`.

zero_point_range

: Range to which the value of the zero point is restricted in fitting to observed magnitudes.

in_place

: If `True`, add the calibrated magnitude to the input table, othewise return a copy.

fit_diff

: If `True`, fit the difference between the instrumental and catalog magnitude instead of the
  treating the catalog mag as the dependent variable.

verbose: bool optional

: If `True`, print additional output.

# Returns

`astropy.table.Table`

: Table containing the calibrated magnitudes and the fit parameters.

methods.name: stellarphot.analysis.transit_fitting.TransitModelFit.setup_model
documentation:
Configure a transit model for fitting. The `duration` and `depth`
are used to estimate underlying fit parameters; they are not
themselves fit parameters.

# Parameters

t0

: Time of the center of the transit. Can be in any units
  but should be consistent with the units for the `period`
  and for the times used for fitting.

depth

: Depth of the transit in parts per thousand.

duration

: Duration of the transit,in the same units as `t0` and
  `period`.

period

: Period of the planet. Should be in the same units as `t0`
  and times used for fitting.

inclination

: Inclination of the orbit, in degrees.

airmass_trend

: Coefficient for a linear trend in airmass.

width_trend

: Coefficient for a linear trend in stellar width.

spp_trend

: Coefficient for a linear trend in sky per pixel.

# Returns

None

: Sets values for the model parameters.

methods.name: stellarphot.analysis.transit_fitting.TransitModelFit.data_light_curve
documentation:
Function to return data light curve, optionally detrended by one or
more parameters.

# Parameters

data

: Data to use for calculating the light curve. If not provided,
  the data used for fitting will be used.

detrend_by

: Parameter(s) to detrend by. If `None`, no detrending is
  done. If `'all'`, all parameters that are set will be
  used for detrending.

# Returns

data

: Data light curve, detrended if requested.

methods.name: stellarphot.analysis.transit_fitting.TransitModelFit.model_light_curve
documentation:
Calculate the light curve corresponding to the model, optionally
detrended by one or more parameters.

# Parameters

at_times

: Times at which to calculate the model. If not provided, the
  times used for fitting will be used.

detrend_by

: Parameter(s) to detrend by. If `None`, no detrending is
  done. If `'all'`, all parameters that are set will be
  used for detrending.

# Returns

model

: Model light curve.

Transit model fits to observed light curves.

# Attributes

airmass

: Airmass at each time. Must be set before fitting.

BIC

: Bayesian Information Criterion for the fit. This is calculated
  after the fit is performed.

data

: Observed fluxes. Must be set before fitting.

model

: The model used for fitting. This is a combination of the batman
  transit model and any other trends that are included in the fit.
  This is set up when the `setup_model` method is called.

n_fit_parameters

: Number of parameters that were fit. This is calculated after the
  fit is performed.

spp

: Sky per pixel at each time. Must be set before fitting.

times

: Times at which the light curve is observed. Must be set before
  fitting.

width

: Width of the star in pixels at each time. Must be set before fitting.

weights

: Weights to use for fitting. If not provided, all weights are
  set to 1.
